element_choice.justification: All elements are semantically correct: <details>/<summary> for expandable FAQs, <ol> for breadcrumb, <a href='tel:'> for phone number, <a> for contact link, <search> for the search landmark, visible <label> for the input, and no <dl> misused for FAQ items. No div-soup or wrong element choices.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used precisely where needed: aria-label on <nav> and <search>, aria-labelledby on <section> elements, aria-current='page' on breadcrumb current item, aria-hidden='true' on decorative emoji. No redundant role attributes or ARIA compensating for non-semantic markup.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Frequently Asked Questions'), three <h2> category headings, and <h2> for 'Still need help?' — no levels skipped, hierarchy correctly reflects document structure.

landmark_structure.justification: Full landmark coverage: <header>, <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> outside <main>, <main> wrapping only page-specific content, <search>, labelled <section> elements, and <footer>. Two skip links ('Skip to main content', 'Skip to search') are the first focusable elements — appropriate for the page's complexity given the prominent search input.

form_semantics.justification: Single search input has a visible <label> ('Search questions') correctly associated via for/id. Appropriate type='search', placeholder supplements rather than replaces the label, and the form sits inside a <search> landmark.

content_realism.justification: All questions and answers are realistic, specific, and helpful — realistic pricing, feature names, support hours, refund policy, and import instructions. No Lorem ipsum or generic placeholders.

list_semantics.justification: Breadcrumb uses <ol> (order matters), FAQ items within each category use <ul> (unordered collection), and a nested <ul> is used inside one answer for plan tiers. All list type choices are appropriate.

expectations_met: <main> wraps only FAQ content — site header, breadcrumb nav, and footer are outside it
Two skip links ('Skip to main content', 'Skip to search') are the first focusable elements
Questions are NOT marked up as <dl>
Contact link is <a>, phone number is <a href='tel:+18005553920'>
Contact info is NOT wrapped in <address> — correct, as it is support contact not site owner/author info
additional_observations: <search> element correctly wraps the search form as a landmark
All <section> elements have aria-labelledby pointing to their visible headings
tabindex='-1' on <main> allows skip link focus to land correctly
aria-hidden='true' on decorative phone emoji is a nice touch
